name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value greater than the original input

name: align_not_identity_when_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_not_identity_when_aligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a % b == 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: If the input is already aligned, align_to_usize should return it unchanged

name: align_result_not_multiple
code:
proof fn phi_3_align_result_not_multiple(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) % b != 0,
{
}
reason: The result of down-alignment must itself be a multiple of the alignment value

name: zero_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_4_zero_not_aligned(size: nat)
    requires
        size > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(0 as nat, size),
{
}
reason: Zero is trivially divisible by any positive number so aligned(0, size) must be true

name: align_and_aligned_disagree
code:
proof fn phi_5_align_and_aligned_disagree(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(align_to_usize(a, b) as nat, b as nat),
{
}
reason: The output of align_to_usize must satisfy the aligned predicate for the same divisor
